response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems engineer with genuine distributed-systems exposure (low-latency micro-services handling millions of requests/second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) and leadership-flavored roles. However, the role centers on C++ at scale for a cloud compute platform, and the resume shows only about six months of C/C++ work at National Instruments plus a thesis project — far short of the required 8 years. The core stack is DLang, Node.js, and TypeScript, with no production cloud compute platform ownership. The C++ and large-scale infrastructure gaps are disqualifying for this specific position.
strengths: Real distributed/low-latency systems work at high request volumes
Long track record with senior/staff and founder-level ownership
Strong testing, TDD, and CI/CD craftsmanship discipline
concerns: Roughly 6 months of C/C++ vs. the 8-year requirement
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale infra leadership
Core experience is web/DLang/Node, not the role's stack
